mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High real short-term assets combined with declining federal funds rates indicate excess liquidity seeking yield, which flows directly into equities and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and operational efficiency, which immediately boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust economic activity and wage income growth, which sustains consumer demand and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.01, or positive returns drop below 45% during payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and tighter credit conditions, which drags down related corporate earnings and produces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative returns, or the signal is overwhelmed by positive technology sector earnings in greater than 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside stagnant real GDP growth indicate stagflationary pressures, which force central banks to maintain restrictive policy and compress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative returns, or equity markets rally despite the signal in greater than 55% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real short-term assets combined with rising housing starts indicate strong domestic demand and healthy credit expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined demand signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the predictive accuracy drops below 40% in walk-forward validation.
